differential_diagnoses: Hepatorenal syndrome with associated intracranial hypertension
next_steps: Perform lumbar puncture with measurement of opening pressure and cerebrospinal fluid analysis (cell count, protein, glucose, culture, cryptococcal antigen, cytology)
Obtain MRI of the brain with magnetic resonance venography (MRV)
Consult ophthalmology for slit-lamp examination and funduscopic assessment for papilledema
Evaluate coagulation profile and platelet function given the cirrhotic state and recent clinical decline
Assess for signs of terlipressin-related adverse effects or systemic hypertension

differential_diagnoses: Spontaneous intracranial hypotension secondary to therapeutic paracentesis
next_steps: Obtain MRI brain with magnetic resonance venography (MRV) immediately
Consult neurology for urgent evaluation and bedside assessment for focal deficits
Perform ophthalmological evaluation (fundoscopy) for papilledema or evidence of increased intracranial pressure
Evaluate coagulation profile (PT/INR, PTT, fibrinogen, platelets) and correct severe coagulopathy if lumbar puncture is required
Perform lumbar puncture with opening pressure measurement, pending stable coagulation status and imaging clearance
Review temporal blood pressure trends in correlation with terlipressin dosing
Send cerebrospinal fluid for cell count, protein, glucose, culture, cytology, and cryptococcal antigen
